title: University of Arizona Launches Largest-Ever Human Rapamycin Trial for Healthy Aging
description: The University of Arizona R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy launched a Phase 3 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial enrolling 720 adults aged 65 and older to evaluate whether weekly low-dose rapamycin (8 mg) slows aging over two years. Primary endpoints are frailty transition and IL-6 (systemic inflammation), with an extensive biomarker panel spanning epigenetic clocks, immune phenotyping, AGE accumulation, lung and joint function, and direct mTOR pathway engagement. This is the most ambitious human rapamycin study ever conducted, designed to definitively establish whether mTOR inhibition can slow clinically meaningful aging trajectories.

title: Lifespan.io Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026: A Wave of Longevity Research Across Senolytics, Reprogramming, NAD+, and Metabolic Aging
description: The April 2026 Rejuvenation Roundup from Lifespan.io aggregated dozens of significant studies: tomatidine emerged as a senotherapeutic improving cognition and reducing senescence in aged mice; a new study found rapamycin may blunt the exercise response in older humans under certain protocols; partial reprogramming mitigated myocardial infarction damage in mice; and a combination NAD+ treatment using NMN plus apigenin restored muscle function and bone structure in aged mice. The EU iHelp epigenetic age feedback study showed 72% of participants reported feeling healthier after one year of personalized biological age feedback plus lifestyle coaching.

title: Endlyz Raises Seed 2 Financing for Root-Cause Parkinson's Neurodegeneration Platform
description: Endlyz Therapeutics closed its Seed 2 financing backed by AbbVie Ventures, the Dementia Discovery Fund, Oxford Science Enterprises, and Parkinson's UK, advancing small-molecule modulators of ATP13A2 and ATP10B to restore lysosomal function and potentially halt Parkinson's disease progression. New collaborations with SandboxAQ for AI-driven drug discovery and University of Oxford researchers further de-risk the platform. Endlyz's lysosomal restoration approach is relevant across neurodegeneration broadly, framing cellular waste clearance as a longevity target.

title: Perplexity Health Launches for 69M+ Medicare Users; AI Health Platforms Scale Longevity Diagnostics
description: Perplexity Health completed its US rollout to Pro and Max subscribers in April 2026, enabling users to connect electronic health records, wearable data, and lab results from 1.7 million providers for personalized AI-driven health insights grounded in clinical guidelines. Concurrently, January AI entered the CMS Medicare App Library, extending predictive glucose and metabolic health tools to 69 million+ Medicare beneficiaries. These launches signal a broad acceleration of AI-native health intelligence into mainstream aging and preventive care.

title: SuperAge Games Debut as World's First Longevity Fitness Competition; Insilico-Lilly $2.75B AI Deal Anchors AI-Driven Aging Drug Discovery
description: Super Age launched the SuperAge Games, a November 2026 New York City competition measuring cardiovascular capacity, grip strength, balance, agility, working memory, and biomarkers as proxies for aging rate, reframing athleticism around healthspan durability. In parallel, Insilico Medicine's $2.75 billion collaboration with Eli Lilly (announced March 29) continued attracting attention as one of the largest AI-to-pharma asset licensing deals in longevity-adjacent drug discovery. Both developments reflect the field's accelerating shift from lab research to consumer and commercial deployment.

title: University of Arizona Launches Phase 3 Rapamycin Trial for Aging (720 Participants)
source: University of Arizona R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy
description: A double-blind, randomized Phase 3 trial will enroll 720 adults aged 65 and older to evaluate weekly low-dose rapamycin (8 mg) over two years, measuring frailty transition and IL-6 as co-primary endpoints alongside epigenetic clocks, immune profiling, AGE accumulation, and lung and joint health assessments. This is the largest and most comprehensively phenotyped human rapamycin study ever conducted and is funded by philanthropist Ken Coit.

title: Endlyz Raises Seed 2 Financing, Leans Into Root-Cause Neuroscience
description: Endlyz Therapeutics secured Seed 2 financing from AbbVie Ventures, Dementia Discovery Fund, Oxford Science Enterprises, and Parkinson's UK to advance ATP13A2/ATP10B small-molecule modulators restoring lysosomal function as a root-cause intervention in Parkinson's and neurodegeneration. New collaborations with SandboxAQ for AI-driven drug discovery and University of Oxford's Professor Richard Wade-Martins further strengthen the pipeline.

title: Super Age Debuts World's First Longevity Fitness Games
description: Super Age announced the SuperAge Games, a healthspan-focused fitness competition scheduled for November 7, 2026 in New York City, testing cardiovascular capacity, grip strength, balance, agility, working memory, and biomarkers. The initiative reframes athletic competition around aging durability rather than peak performance, with a multi-month training journey and immersive programming.

title: Lifespan.io Rejuvenation Roundup April 2026
description: The April 2026 monthly roundup covered tomatidine as a cognitive-improving senotherapeutic, the rapamycin-exercise blunting interaction in older humans, partial reprogramming reducing myocardial infarction damage, NAD+ combination therapy restoring muscle and bone in aged mice, the EU iHelp one-year epigenetic feedback results, and Insilico Medicine's $2.75B Eli Lilly collaboration for AI-driven drug discovery.

title: Perplexity Health Launches for US Users, Integrating EHR and Wearable Data for Personalized Health AI
description: Perplexity Health launched for US Pro and Max subscribers, connecting electronic health records from 1.7 million providers, wearable data, and lab results through b.well Connected Health's network, with responses grounded in clinical guidelines and peer-reviewed journals. From a longevity perspective, the platform enables individuals to track aging biomarkers, identify risks earlier, and make more informed decisions about healthspan interventions.

title: APOE4 Alzheimer's Risk Gene Reveals Hidden Bone Quality Decline Specifically in Women
source: Longevity.Technology / Buck Institute for Research on Aging
description: Buck Institute researchers found that female mice carrying APOE4 show markedly fragile bones despite appearing structurally normal on imaging, due to APOE4 suppression of perilacunar/canalicular remodeling. The study, published in Advanced Science, suggests osteocyte dysfunction could serve as an early biological sentinel for neurodegenerative risk in women, proposing bone quality as a novel aging biomarker preceding Alzheimer's symptoms.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Announce $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Collaboration
description: Insilico Medicine announced a global R&D collaboration with Eli Lilly valued at up to $2.75 billion, including a $115 million upfront payment, granting Lilly exclusive worldwide licenses to preclinical oral therapeutics discovered by Insilico's Pharma.AI platform. The deal marks an escalation from software licensing to actual asset transfer, validating AI-driven longevity drug discovery at major pharma scale.

title: Smith+Nephew AGILI-C Knee Implant Outperforms Standard Care at 5 Years in Osteoarthritis
description: Five-year data from Smith+Nephew's FDA-approved AGILI-C scaffold knee implant showed superior pain reduction and mobility outcomes compared to standard care in patients with mild-to-moderate osteoarthritis, addressing a major treatment gap for patients not qualifying for total knee replacement. CPT reimbursement is expected in 2027, potentially broadening access significantly.

title: EU iHelp Study: Epigenetic Age Feedback Drives Sustained Lifestyle Change at One Year
description: One-year results from the EU iHelp project, involving 178 adults receiving personalized reports combining epigenetic clock estimates, polygenic cancer risk scores, and Fitbit data, showed 72.5% reported feeling healthier, 60.4% increased physical activity, and 47.3% improved diet. The study recommends Zhang2019-BLUP as a stable, interpretable clock for public-facing engagement interventions.

title: January AI Enters Medicare App Library, Reaching 69 Million+ Patients
description: January AI, a precision health app providing personalized glucose predictions and metabolic insights, became one of the first third-party applications in the newly launched CMS Medicare App Library, extending AI-driven metabolic health monitoring to over 69 million Medicare beneficiaries. The integration represents a significant step in scaling preventive and longevity-relevant health tools into the senior population at national scale.

title: Tomatidine Identified as Senotherapeutic Improving Cognition and Reducing Senescence in Aged Mice
description: A study published in Aging Cell showed that tomatidine, a steroidal alkaloid from green tomatoes, acts as a senotherapeutic compound that reduces cellular senescence, enhances tight junction protein expression preserving blood-brain barrier integrity, and improves cognitive function in aged mice. Tomatidine also diminished brain endothelial cell senescence, adding it to a growing class of natural senolytic/senomorphic candidates.

title: Young Microbiome Transplantation Prevents Liver Cancer and Reverses Multiple Aging Hallmarks in Mice
description: Researchers found that fecal microbiota transplantation restoring a youthful gut microbiome in aged mice prevented liver cancer formation in all treated animals (0/8 versus 2/8 in controls), while reversing inflammation, fibrosis, mitochondrial decline, telomere attrition, and DNA damage at both molecular and functional levels. The study identifies gut microbiome rejuvenation as a multi-hallmark aging intervention with broad translational potential.

title: Ocugen OCU410 Gene Therapy Shows 46% Lesion Reduction in Geographic Atrophy Phase 2 Trial
description: Ocugen's OCU410 gene-agnostic modifier gene therapy for dry age-related macular degeneration showed a 46% reduction in macular lesion growth at 12 months across medium and high dose groups in the Phase 2 ArMaDa trial, with no serious treatment-related adverse events. Ocugen is targeting Phase 3 initiation in Q3 2026, positioning OCU410 as a potential treatment for geographic atrophy regardless of specific genetic mutation.

title: SIRIO Europe Launches Aeion Habit-First Longevity Supplement Platform at Vitafoods Europe 2026
description: Contract development and manufacturing organization SIRIO Europe launched Aeion, a longevity supplement platform with eight ready-to-market concepts targeting energy, mobility, heart health, metabolism, and inflammaging in novel delivery formats including Snowmelt powders, Fizzybit tablets, and LifeChews jelly tablets. The platform debuts at Vitafoods Europe 2026 (May 5-7, Barcelona) and is designed to translate aging science into repeatable daily consumer rituals.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Projected to Reach $8-9 Billion in Full-Year 2026
description: Q1 2026 longevity biotech financing reached $3.74 billion across 49 events, a 56% increase over Q1 2025's $2.40 billion, driven by epigenetic reprogramming and cell therapy platforms. Full-year projections estimate $8-9 billion total, representing 55-60% growth over 2025's $5.72 billion base, signaling a structural shift in longevity capital flows toward later-stage and scaled platforms.

title: Function Health Acquires Getlabs to Scale At-Home Longevity Testing
description: Function Health acquired Getlabs, a mobile healthcare platform dispatching licensed phlebotomists for at-home lab testing, to extend its comprehensive biomarker testing to patients who cannot attend clinic visits. The acquisition positions Function to make health data collection accessible in everyday settings, scaling longevity diagnostics including metabolic, hormonal, and aging biomarker panels to a broader population.

title: Viking Therapeutics Completes Enrollment in Phase 3 VANQUISH-2 Trial of Dual GLP-1/GIP Agonist VK2735
description: Viking Therapeutics completed enrollment of approximately 1,000 adults with type 2 diabetes and obesity in the Phase 3 VANQUISH-2 trial of subcutaneous VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist, with a primary endpoint of percent body weight reduction at 78 weeks. Phase 2 VENTURE showed up to 14.7% body weight reduction in 13 weeks, and researchers increasingly frame GLP-1 class drugs as the first metabolic longevity therapeutics.

title: piRNA Blood Test Predicts Two-Year Survival with 86% Accuracy in Older Adults
description: Duke University researchers identified six PIWI-interacting RNAs (piRNAs) in blood that predict two-year survival in adults aged 71 and older with up to 86% accuracy, outperforming age, cholesterol, physical activity, and 180+ other clinical measures. A virtual clinical trial simulation showed that therapeutic manipulation of piRNA levels could meaningfully improve survival across two-, five-, and ten-year timeframes, opening a new avenue for longevity biomarker development.

title: Nature Briefing: Partial Cellular Reprogramming Enters Human Clinical Trials in 2026
description: Nature reported that partial reprogramming therapy using targeted protein expression to reverse cellular aging is entering human clinical trials in 2026 for the first time, with Life Biosciences testing OSK-based ER-100 in optic neuropathy patients. Researchers cite potential to eventually rejuvenate entire organs or the whole body, while acknowledging significant risks including potential cellular identity loss or oncogenic transformation.

title: NADMED Awards $30,000 Grant to Cleveland Clinic Organ Preservation and NAD+ Metabolism Research
description: Finnish biotech NADMED selected Cleveland Clinic's Schlegel Lab as the first recipient of its $30,000 inaugural award, supporting research on metabolic injury during organ preservation and NAD+ redox dynamics in transplanted organs. The work has implications for improving organ viability in transplantation and understanding mitochondrial and metabolic aging at the cellular level.

title: Loyal LOY-002 Anti-Aging Dog Drug Nears FDA Conditional Approval for 2026 Launch
description: Loyal's LOY-002, a daily oral prescription pill targeting IGF-1 to slow biological aging in dogs aged 10 and older weighing at least 14 pounds, has completed two of three FDA conditional approval requirements (safety and efficacy) and is targeting market launch in 2026. If approved, LOY-002 would become the first FDA-authorized drug to slow aging in any species, establishing a regulatory template for longevity therapeutics.

title: Peter Attia Research Worth Sharing April 2026: Paternal Exercise Epigenetics and Autonomic Aging Theory
description: Attia's April 2026 research digest highlighted an animal study showing paternal endurance exercise confers fitness advantages to male offspring via sperm microRNAs and PGC-1 alpha, and a position paper proposing sympathetic-parasympathetic nervous system dysregulation as a unifying framework for aging hallmarks including mitochondrial dysfunction and epigenetic alterations. The autonomic aging theory offers a new multi-system lens for intervention design.

title: Healthy Aging 2026 Conference at Stanford University on May 4
description: The Longevity Project's annual Healthy Aging 2026 conference is scheduled for May 4, 2026 at Stanford University, bringing together leading researchers and practitioners in nutrition, fitness, sleep science, and mindfulness to present the latest evidence-based longevity research. The conference is available for in-person and online attendance, reflecting growing mainstream institutional interest in aging science.

title: Extracellular Matrix Aging Clock Using Circulating Matrisome Proteins Predicts Disease and Aging
description: A new biological clock based on circulating extracellular matrix (ECM) proteins was validated as a sensitive biomarker of biological aging and disease risk, outperforming standard clinical measures in predicting aging trajectories. The findings suggest that targeting ECM remodeling may offer new therapeutic strategies for promoting healthy aging, given the ECM's role in tissue repair, inflammation, and cellular signaling.

title: Longevity Market to Hit $67 Billion by 2035 as Employer and Healthcare Business Models Evolve
description: Analysis from Longevity.Technology projects the global longevity market will grow to $67 billion by 2035, driven by employer adoption of healthspan screening programs and emerging business models that convert longevity science into workplace productivity tools. Panel discussions at Longevity Show 2026 previews highlighted absenteeism and productivity data as the key metrics converting skeptical corporate buyers.
